title: Consent Continuity Cannot Be Reconstructed
scenario: Clinical records indicate that consent may have existed, but the system cannot reconstruct the consent scope, timing, signer, or applicability before execution.
initial_state: A historical consent reference exists in the clinical record.
state_transition: Required consent reconstruction evidence is missing at execution evaluation.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before relying on the historical consent reference.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent continuity requires reconstructable evidence of consent scope, authority, timing, and applicability.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the consent basis cannot be reconstructed from available evidence.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when consent continuity cannot be reconstructed.
clinical_action: Execute clinical action based on unreconstructable consent reference
